learning_outcomes.description: Describe the structure and function of the endocrine system and pregnancy related disorders that may be related to endocrine dysfunction.
Identify the various pathologies associated with structures and functions of endocrine, cardiovascular, nervous and gastrointestinal systems

Examine the physiological changes associated with the nervous, endocrine, cardiovascular and gastrointestinal systems during pregnancy. Also demonstrate an understanding of how disruption of processes related to these systems may lead to pregnancy disorders.
Understand anatomical and physiological roles of the placenta during pregnancy
Recognise and apply the principles that determine the quality use of medicines to patient therapy in healthcare
Demonstrate competence in the therapeutic use of drugs, drug safety and the use of drugs in their professional fields
Explain the pharmacology and therapeutics relevant to drug use during pregnancy and birth
Apply basic observational and experimental techniques in human physiology
Demonstrate an understanding of common congenital disorders and adverse birth outcomes
Understand the anatomical and physiological adaptations of the Puerperium

assessments.task_description:
In-semester examination will cover the content of lectures delivered in weeks 1 to 4. This exam will include multiple choice and short answer questions and will be help on campus during your standard lecture times (in either your lecture theatre or a suitable alternative venue)

The exam will be a closed book invigilated exam held on campus, undertaken via the Inspera eAssessment platform. Students will be required to bring a laptop to the exam that meets the device requirements for Inspera. It is important that you check before the exam that your laptop meets the device requirements for using the Inspera Assessment platform. If you do not own a suitable laptop, you can borrow one from the Library. The library website Get familiar with Inspera provides the latest information for students about using Inspera. Further information about the exam will be provided on Blackboard. 

Have your UQ student ID card available for all your exams. 

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.due_date: These 5 quizzes will be completed in class as part of the 2 prac classes and 3 workshops. See the timetable for your scheduled class times.
assessments.category: Participation/ Student contribution, Practical/ Demonstration, Quiz
assessments.task_description:
In each of the two practical classes and three workshop classes, you will be expected to complete an in‑class quiz. These quizzes may take different forms depending on the nature of the practical class or workshop. Each quiz will be assessed on a pass/fail basis. If you achieve 50% or more, you will be awarded full marks. If you achieve less than 50%, you will be awarded 0 marks.

You will receive a total score out of 20%, based on your best four scores from the five quizzes (5% per quiz, to a maximum of 20%).

Further assessment details will be provided on Blackboard during the semester.

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.assessment_title: Fact Sheet on one maternal disorder
assessments.task_description:
Create a Fact Sheet on one maternal disorder during pregnancy. Details of the format of the assessment will be available on Blackboard.

Submission of this assessment is required to be submitted in both PDF and Word format through the Turnitin Link in Blackboard. Details of this assessment will be provided on Blackboard prior to the assessment due date. 

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You must submit your Assessment task, with the electronic coversheet available on the School's website (under Undergraduate - Assessment), to Turnitin by the submission deadline. You may submit drafts to Turnitin up to the due date, but you will only be able to submit once on or after the due date. You should also keep an electronic copy of every piece of assessment you submit.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 3(23 Feb - 15 Mar)
learning_activities.topic:
Lecture series- Biomedical Adaptations in pregnancy [Cuffe]

Module 1: Biomedical Adaptations in pregnancy

This module will provide you with a solid understanding of the anatomical, physiological, metabolic, endocrine and immune changes that occur throughout pregnancy, how all other systems in the body are changed by pregnancy and how pregnancy complications such as gestational diabetes might develop, also include lectures focused on therapeutics in pregnancy.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Lecture series- Biomedical Adaptations in pregnancy [Cuffe] Module 1: Biomedical Adaptations in pregnancy This module will provide you with a solid understanding of the anatomical, physiological, metabolic, endocrine and immune changes that occur throughout pregnancy, how all other systems in the body are changed by pregnancy and how pregnancy complications such as gestational diabetes might develop, also include lectures focused on therapeutics in pregnancy.

learning_activities.topic:
Introduction to the course [Cuffe]

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07, L08, L09, L10

Anatomical changes in pregnancy & pathology[Cuffe]

This practical class will focus on making sure students understand how the body changes during pregnancy. This class will also include specimens allowing students to learn more about key pathologies that can occur related to pregnancy.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L08
learning_activities.topic_description: Anatomical changes in pregnancy & pathology[Cuffe] This practical class will focus on making sure students understand how the body changes during pregnancy. This class will also include specimens allowing students to learn more about key pathologies that can occur related to pregnancy.

learning_activities.topic:
Gestational Diabetes Mellitus practical[Cuffe]

Students will be asked to fast before coming to class before they measure blood glucose before and after consuming a glucose load. This practical class involves students undertaking a modified version of the OGTT that all pregnant woman undergo. Students will learn how this process detects GDM and why glucose control is disrupted in pregnancy

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L08
learning_activities.topic_description: Gestational Diabetes Mellitus practical[Cuffe] Students will be asked to fast before coming to class before they measure blood glucose before and after consuming a glucose load. This practical class involves students undertaking a modified version of the OGTT that all pregnant woman undergo. Students will learn how this process detects GDM and why glucose control is disrupted in pregnancy

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 4 To Week 7(16 Mar - 19 Apr)
learning_activities.topic:
Lecture series- Cardiovascular development in pregnancy and lactation [Cuffe], [Clifton], [Isaacs]

Module 2:Cardiovascular development in pregnancy and lactation 

This module will build on your current anatomical and physiological knowledge of the cardiovascular system and focus on complications that relate to this system. This module will also include lectures related to breast development and lactation.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06
learning_activities.topic_title: Lecture series- Cardiovascular development in pregnancy and lactation [Cuffe], [Clifton], [Isaacs]
learning_activities.topic_description: Lecture series- Cardiovascular development in pregnancy and lactation [Cuffe], [Clifton], [Isaacs] Module 2: Cardiovascular development in pregnancy and lactation This module will build on your current anatomical and physiological knowledge of the cardiovascular system and focus on complications that relate to this system. This module will also include lectures related to breast development and lactation.

learning_activities.topic:
Postpartum haemorrhage [Clifton]

This face to face workshop will focus on blood loss and hypovolemia related to childbirth. Following an introduction on the topic, students will work through a series of questions related to current Australian guidelines with some guidance provided. Discussion around clinical experiences will be included

Learning outcomes: L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Postpartum haemorrhage [Clifton] This face to face workshop will focus on blood loss and hypovolemia related to childbirth. Following an introduction on the topic, students will work through a series of questions related to current Australian guidelines with some guidance provided. Discussion around clinical experiences will be included

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 8 To Week 9(20 Apr - 03 May)
learning_activities.topic:
Lecture series- Gastrointestinal disorders, depression and asthma in pregnancy [Clifton]

Module 3: Gastrointestinal disorders in pregnancy, depression and Asthma

This module will include GIT disorders of pregnancy as well as asthma and depression


Learning outcomes: L03, L07
learning_activities.topic_title: Lecture series- Gastrointestinal disorders, depression and asthma in pregnancy [Clifton]
learning_activities.topic_description: Lecture series- Gastrointestinal disorders, depression and asthma in pregnancy [Clifton] Module 3: Gastrointestinal disorders in pregnancy, depression and Asthma This module will include GIT disorders of pregnancy as well as asthma and depression

learning_activities.topic:
Lecture series- Pain, Headaches and epilepsy in pregnancy [Borges]

Module 4: Headaches and epilepsy in pregnancy

This module will include lectures on headaches and epilepsy in pregnancy


Learning outcomes: L03
learning_activities.topic_title: Lecture series- Pain, Headaches and epilepsy in pregnancy [Borges]
learning_activities.topic_description: Lecture series- Pain, Headaches and epilepsy  in pregnancy [Borges] Module 4: Headaches and epilepsy in pregnancy This module will include lectures on headaches and epilepsy in pregnancy

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 11 To Week 13(11 May - 31 May)
learning_activities.topic:
Lecture series- Fetal and New-born development [Cuffe]

Module 5: Fetal and New-born development

This module will build on what you learnt in first year about embryonic development and focus on common challenges and complications related to the fetus. This module will also focus on the extreme physiological adaptations the the new-born undergoes to transition to the external environment.


Learning outcomes: L07, L09, L10
learning_activities.topic_description: Lecture series- Fetal and New-born development [Cuffe] Module 5: Fetal and New-born development This module will build on what you learnt in first year about embryonic development and focus on common challenges and complications related to the fetus. This module will also focus on the extreme physiological adaptations the the new-born undergoes to transition to the external environment.

learning_activities.topic:
Pain physiology in labour [Borges]

This workshop will focus on understanding different types of pain and how medications are used to manage pain. This workshop will include a range of principles that apply to all types of pain but will also include concepts specific to pain management in labour.

Learning outcomes: L03, L07, L08
learning_activities.topic_description: Pain physiology in labour [Borges] This workshop will focus on understanding different types of pain and how medications are used to manage pain. This workshop will include a range of principles that apply to all types of pain but will also include concepts specific to pain management in labour.

learning_activities.topic:
Integration workshop (Cuffe)

This face to face workshop will bring together many of the ideas learned throughout the course and focus on understanding what goes wrong in pregnancy when complications do occur. This workshop will include a number of activities relating various symptoms to the pathophysiology underlying conditions that occur throughout pregnancy.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07, L08
learning_activities.topic_description: Integration workshop (Cuffe) This face to face workshop will bring together many of the ideas learned throughout the course and focus on understanding what goes wrong in pregnancy when complications do occur.
